title: Crypto fraud complaints - which agency actually has jurisdiction
p1:
Lost money in a crypto scam? The biggest mistake people make is reporting it to the wrong place first.

Many assume there is one “crypto police” agency that handles every fraud case. The reality is more complicated.
p2: Different agencies handle different types of complaints. A fake investment platform, a hacked account, a stolen wallet, and a misleading crypto advertisement may fall under different authorities. One surprising fact: a financial regulator may investigate a company’s behavior, while a law enforcement agency may handle criminal activity.
p3:
The practical step is to first identify what happened: Was it theft, fraud, a scam investment, identity misuse, or a platform issue? The right complaint channel depends on the facts, not just the word “crypto.”

Reporting quickly, preserving transaction records, wallet addresses, messages, and payment details can make a major difference.
conclusion: Have you ever wondered which agency should receive a crypto fraud complaint first, or have you seen people report these cases to the wrong place?
